behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is model-output quality plus policy wiring: a fake classifier can always return "explicit" and miss real misclassification on actual explicit images. REAL-INFRA is not the key requirement here; LLM-DEP is. Rejected higher level Workflow because behavior text covers a single model judgment path (image classification) and one decision outcome, not a multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run moderation end-to-end for image posts with a real vision moderation model on a curated explicit-image set, then assert each post is marked held-for-review and never enters the publish path.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: This behavior targets robustness of semantic detection under obfuscation, which depends on real model behavior. Stubs/mocks hide this failure by returning predetermined labels. Rejected Workflow because behavior text specifies one classifier capability (text moderation), not multiple agentic stages chained together.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real text moderation model against a corpus of obfuscated hate-speech variants (leet, homoglyphs, mixed unicode) and verify each sample is flagged and routed to human review.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure is module handoff/orchestration (image result + text result + decision + publish/hold routing). Deterministic pass responses from both checkers are sufficient to expose silent-drop/incorrect-routing bugs. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required by behavior text, (b) no dependence on model-quality output because checks are defined as already passing, (c) no required multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Wire moderation service, decision logic, and publish/hold sinks in-process with stubbed checkers returning PASS; submit posts and assert publish sink receives exactly one record and hold/drop sinks receive none.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires end-to-end timing across multiple sequential AI checks plus final decision. MULTI-STEP applies: image and text model calls are distinct agentic steps in one pipeline. Mocked steps can hide real latency accumulation and contention. Rejected lower System/Integration because the concrete text requires the full multi-step moderation pipeline, not a single external boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real submission-to-decision runs through the full pipeline (real image model call, real text model call, real decision/publish path) under peak-like concurrent load; assert p95 end-to-end latency per submission is <= 5s.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: Failure is error-propagation and fallback routing between moderation caller, error handler, and retry queue. A deterministic dependency stub that returns timeout/unavailable errors can catch incorrect auto-approve behavior. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) real OS/network is not required by behavior text, (b) no dependence on model output quality, (c) no multi-agentic chain required.
behaviors.test_description: Run service + queue components together in-process; make vision dependency return unavailable/timeout errors and verify posts land in retry queue with no publish approval event emitted.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: Borderline consistency risk is tied to real model variability near threshold; stubs produce stable labels and can hide flips. The behavior also checks repeated real decisions over time window, so real model calls are needed to surface nondeterministic drift. Rejected Workflow because it focuses on repeated outcomes of one image moderation path, not a multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image repeatedly within the defined short window using the real vision moderation model and production decision path; assert final moderation outcome is stable across repeats (or consistently resolved via intended cache/stickiness rule).
